hypothesis: If a closed-loop Bayesian optimization platform autonomously explores the multi-dimensional parameter space of reactive carbon electrolyzers (including electrolyte concentration, temperature, flow rate, and applied current density), then it will identify non-intuitive optimal parameter combinations that achieve higher CO2 reactor yields than those attainable through conventional researcher-guided experimentation, because Gaussian process regression can efficiently model complex, nonlinear interactions among experimental variables to guide sequential experiment selection toward global optima.
